facts.context: Which tool will best help scientists understand relationships between oceans and climate changes?
Human activity most likely contributes to which of the following changes on Earth?
facts.truth: an increase in the amount of atmospheric carbon dioxide
distractors: an increase in the length of a day
a decrease in the number of volcanic eruptions
a decrease in the magnitude of large earthquakes

facts.context: Scientists are developing new satellite technology to help predict the weather. How will developing this technology most likely benefit people?
facts.truth: It will help warn people about dangerous storms.
distractors: It will show people that storms can be prevented.
It will show people that science is interesting.
It will help people drive in the rain.

facts.context: Some students have gathered a collection of rock samples from the school yard to study. They scraped the rocks against a piece of quartz from the school lab. Which property of minerals are the students investigating?

distractors: the Sun's rotation on its axis

facts.context: Which is the best explanation of why the Moon revolves around Earth?
facts.truth: The Moon is attracted by the gravity of Earth.
distractors: The Moon has a thinner atmosphere than Earth.

The Moon has a smaller gravitational force than Earth.

facts.context: Which of these best describes the relationship between Earth's layers?
facts.truth: The hottest layers are closest to the core.
distractors: The more liquid layers are closest to the crust.

The more metallic layers are closest to the crust.

facts.truth: The water changes into water vapor.

facts.context: George is studying erosion. He learns how rocks break apart over time. Which of these most likely forms when a rock weathers and breaks into small pieces?

facts.truth: the planet to revolve once around the Sun
distractors: the seasons to complete a full cycle
the planet to rotate once on its axis

facts.context: Three gases found in Earth's atmosphere are carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and oxygen. What is their order of abundance from greatest to least?

Which of the following is the main reason water at the surface of the ocean is warmer than water at the bottom of the ocean?
facts.truth: Water at the surface of the ocean absorbs more energy from the Sun.
distractors: Water at the bottom of the ocean contains more dissolved solids.
Friction is created by fast moving currents at the surface of the ocean.

facts.context: Many scientists accept the theory that excess emission of carbon dioxide from cars and industry causes a layer of gas in the upper atmosphere that traps heat. This, in turn, causes the average temperature on Earth to rise. Which of the following observations supports that theory?
facts.truth: Carbon dioxide in the upper atmosphere reflects heat radiated from Earth's surface.
distractors: The summer of 1998 was one of the hottest summer seasons on record.

facts.context: The early Greeks are credited with many valid concepts in astronomy. Some of their theories were correct; some were later proven incorrect. One theory was that Earth was the center of the universe and that other planets circled Earth. The Greeks thought Earth did not move because its movement was not obvious from the surface of the planet. The Greeks also believed that an invisible sphere surrounding our planet contained the stars. This sphere rotated, explaining the apparent movement of constellations over time. Early Greeks also developed theories about the relationship between the moon and Earth. Why is the same side of the moon always visible from Earth?
facts.truth: The period of rotation and the period of revolution for the moon are about the same.
distractors: The far side of the moon does not reflect sunlight.
The moon stays in one location while Earth revolves around it.
The moon rotates clockwise on its axis while Earth rotates counterclockwise on its axis.

facts.context: An earthquake is caused by sudden shifts in which of the following layers of Earth?
